title: name param on SecretStore trait is public API documented as 'ignored'
description: Scenario: a contributor or user reads the SecretStore trait signature, sees name: &str, writes code that passes a meaningful name expecting it to select a secret — and gets the wrong value silently, because all current backends ignore it and only CachingStore uses it as a cache key. The doc now says 'pass an empty string or any stable identifier' which is correct but unusual. Every new backend PR will have to be corrected when the author naturally uses name as the secret path. The design is fossilized as public API at v0.x: changing the signature is a breaking change. Maintenance consequence: recurring contributor PRs using name as path selector; recurring user issues wondering why get(my_name) returns the wrong secret. Decide before 1.0: either make the parameter do something (multi-secret backends, path override), rename it to _key for cache use, or remove it from the trait and make CachingStore manage keys internally.
